info.title: av_microservice_utils
info.description: Microservice containing a number of handy end-points that can be used by other micro-services or 3rd-parties

get.summary: Returns the associated mobile network operator.
get.description: Returns the Mobile Network Operator this MSISDN is registered on.

get.summary: Returns a random MSISDN based on selection criteria.
get.description: Returns a random MSISDN based on the constraints given in the query parameters. Typically, the endpoint will be called with a single number range: Example: /getRandomMSISDN?numberRanges=600000000..929999999&countryPrefix=27 → 27623108888 (See * below) Note: One can even add multiple ranges to (with equal probability) select a number from the complete set defined by these ranges: /getRandomMSISDN?numberRanges=600000000..639999999,720000000..749999999&countryPrefix=27 Finally, one can limit the number to that belonging to a certain MNO /getRandomMSISDN?numberRanges=600000000..929999999&countryPrefix=27&forMNO=MTN Current available options: MTN, VODACOM, CELLC, or TELKMOBL * IMPORTANT NOTE: The routine uses the java.security.SecureRandom implementation from Oracle, (see explanation). This algorithm produces a statistically random number in the range of the java int primitive. It is thus not possible to generate a number larger than 2,147,483,647. For this reason, it is advised to define the number ranges to the right-most 9 digits.

parameters.name: numberRanges
parameters.description:
Ranges are in the form of
nnnnnn..mmmmmm, oooooo..pppppp

Numerous ranges can be defined, separated by commas.

Example:
numberRangges=830000000..880000000,720000000..729999999

parameters.name: countryPrefix
parameters.description:
Number/String to prepend the generated number with.

Example: countryPrefix=27

parameters.name: forMNO
parameters.description:
If present, will only return a random number from set Mobile Network Operator

Values:
MTN, VODACOM, CELLC, or TELKMOBL

trait_queryParamAdvanceid_advanceId.name: advanceId
trait_queryParamAdvanceid_advanceId.description: Unique identifier of new Advance (/addAdvance) or which advance to repay (/addRepayment)

trait_queryParamLoanamount_loanAmt.name: loanAmt
trait_queryParamLoanamount_loanAmt.description:
Amount borrowed by subscriber OR
Amount being allocated to repaying the Loan (/addRepayment)

trait_queryParamDenomlistid_denomListId.name: denomListId
trait_queryParamDenomlistid_denomListId.description: Which list of denominations should be displayed?

trait_queryParamLoandate_loanDate.name: loanDate
trait_queryParamLoandate_loanDate.description:
Timestamp of loan taken
yyyy/mm/dd HH:mm:ss

trait_queryParamRepaydate_repayDate.name: repayDate
trait_queryParamRepaydate_repayDate.description:
Timestamp of loan repaid
yyyy/mm/dd HH:mm:ss

trait_queryParamServiceamt_serviceAmt.name: serviceAmt
trait_queryParamServiceamt_serviceAmt.description: Amount being allocated to the service fee

trait_queryParamServiceid_serviceId.name: serviceId
trait_queryParamServiceid_serviceId.description: This is the service ID which will indicate </br>1 - Airtime Once-off; </br>2 - Airtime Auto_Topup and </br>3 - Data

trait_queryParamStatusid_statusId.name: statusId
trait_queryParamStatusid_statusId.description:
2 - Confirmed advance/payment
3 - Cancelled advance/payment
6 -  Queued advance/payment

trait_queryParamTimestamp_timestamp.name: timestamp
trait_queryParamTimestamp_timestamp.description:
Timestamp of the event, comprising Date AND Time.
E.g. 2016/09/02 11:10:02
